Transcrição Completa: 19 - Day 19

 

[00:00] Day number 19. So, create a follow-up. Let's bust out your phone. You got your Excel sheet. You got all your numbers. Time to make some follow-up videos.

 

[00:08] So, you're going to take it out, open up your camera app, go ahead and get your selfie video on. Once again, good lighting, good background, nice environment. We're going to go through a few quick videos that you can use that are going to be data driven.

 

[00:22] Now, keep in mind if you've already looked at all of the data that you can find, such as Instagram analytics, such as Facebook analytics, such as everything you can find on their Twitter, YouTube, on socialblade.com, on the spy food data.

 

[00:37] If you're somebody who wants to do SEO or PPC, if you manage to find one of those tools that I told you you can Google that's going to show you every single Facebook ad that anybody's ever run by typing in a search, then I'm not going to be able to tell you the name of, you can go Google that.

 

[00:52] And if you so happen to find one of those tools, another great example of something to follow up with, if you're on their website, their website obviously looks like something you can recreate.

 

[01:02] If their website doesn't have a Facebook pixel, if their website doesn't have an SSL. There are a lot of different ways that you can have a computer nearby, or that you can simply say the specific pain points that you've already called out.

 

[01:15] I personally like to use images, visuals, some type of graphics. I like to keep a laptop nearby. So, that's another example of an asset that you can keep handy.

 

[01:24] And the specific asset such as a laptop, iPad, whatever it is that you have handy, anything, desktop, you want to have up on there whatever it is that you want to show them for that specific piece of data you want to call out.

 

[01:36] So, if you want to call out their Instagram analytics, you're going to want to show on SocialBlade the Instagram analytics or maybe just open up Instagram, right?

 

[01:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Case Study: Certified Whale"

 

NÚMEROS: 193 follow-up attempts, $15K deal

 

VISUAL: Exemplo de cliente de alto valor (whale) que fechou após múltiplas tentativas de follow-up, demonstrando persistência e criatividade no processo de vendas

 

[01:47] So, I had this guy, he was a certified whale that I put up on my board there that I had harpooned, brought into the boat, sank for a 15k deal. So, not a big whale, but a whale nonetheless.

 

[02:00] This specific whale required 193 different follow-up attempts. And on the way, I had learned so many different types of creative follow-up, even beyond my own imagination, because that's exactly what you have to do in the follow-up process. You have to get creative.

 

[02:17] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Gary Vee Client Case Study"

 

NÚMEROS: $25K investment, 200-300 views per post

 

VISUAL: Exemplo de cliente trabalhando com agência de alto nível (Gary Vee) mas obtendo resultados insatisfatórios, demonstrando oportunidade de mostrar falhas da concorrência

 

[02:19] So, one of the cool ways that I did that was each individual painpoint from a datadriven perspective. So, this specific person was working with a bigname agency owner who you guys might know and follow and his name's Gary Vee.

 

[02:33] And Gary Vee was working with this particular client and this particular client that I have had fired Gary Vee because I'd called him out so much on the fact that Gary Vee he was having somebody pay 25K to Gary's company and they were creating content for this guy.

 

[02:49] And the content that was being created it was being distributed in Instagram but yet in real time those posts were getting 200 views or 300 views and I connected the dots.

 

[03:00] I remembered all the videos where I'd seen Gary talking about things like, "No, each view matters and like only the one to, you know, doing things for 200 views matters."

 

[03:10] But yet, that's a garbage thing to say in 2017 and onward whenever you're watching this because in real time, you can take any post and you can do what's called a boost. You can be a master in paid traffic.

 

[03:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Instagram Growth Strategy"

 

NÚMEROS: 30 hashtags, 3 initial hashtags

 

VISUAL: Ferramenta displaypurposes.com, sistema de hashtags trending, estratégia de explore page, targeting de followers

 

[03:25] You can literally do things like add the right hashtags behind it, up to 30 of them, that you can use on a tool called displaypurposes.com. And when you go into displaypurposes.com, you can type in three different hashtags.

 

[03:38] And those three different hashtags, if they're big, broad hashtags that relate to whatever you're going to post about, it will populate 30 real-time hashtags that are trending or have opportunity or probability to make you show up in the explore page.

 

[03:54] And target your that piece of content you just posted or that your client's going to post directly towards the people who match that kind of targeting option within your own follower list to make it more likely for that piece of content to show up to more of your followers.

 

[04:10] Instagram's a tricky place, but when you don't know what you don't know, it takes advantage of it. You got to show your clients these things.

 

[04:17] Bust out your phone, open up the camera app, get your piece of data ready. This is an example of something I use.

 

[04:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Follow-up Video Script Template"

 

NÚMEROS: N/A

 

VISUAL: Script de vídeo de follow-up data-driven, abordagem não-vendedora, apresentação de dados de forma transparente e imparcial

 

[04:25] So, hey Bob, what's going on? So, I know I sent you that email. You know, it may had that video or that data driven email. And I know it was a lot and I know that I had already sent you a video and we're in this process together right now.

 

[04:40] And I really want to show you once again transparently some stuff about you in a very unbiased perspective. So, this this piece of data that I want to show you, I can't tell you how important it is and I'm not even going to try to express with my own words, otherwise it looks like I'm trying to sell you.

 

[04:58] So, I've brought up other results or examples of results or different case studies and testimonials of people, places, and things that right now in real time are getting results doing what it is you're not doing.

 

[05:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Facebook Pixel Importance"

 

NÚMEROS: N/A

 

VISUAL: Demonstração visual do Facebook Pixel, coleta de tráfego, benefícios de implementação mesmo sem conhecimento de Facebook Ads

 

[05:12] For example, like just having this Facebook pixel right here is a good way for you in real time to get more traffic collected. Even if you don't understand Facebook ads, even if you don't have somebody doing it, it doesn't matter.

 

[05:24] Put the pixel on the site because when the pixel's there, you're able to collect the traffic that you wouldn't otherwise be able to collect.

 

[05:31] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SSL Certificate Critical Issue"

 

NÚMEROS: N/A

 

VISUAL: Ausência de certificado SSL no website, vulnerabilidade de segurança, impacto no ranking do Google

 

[05:33] Hear me out. You don't even have an SSL certificate on your website. Look at this. I found this YouTube video. It's called a DDoS attack. And I wanted to visualize exactly what it is that you see right here happening. Check this out.

 

[05:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "DDoS Attack Visualization"

 

NÚMEROS: 1 million+ simultaneous hits

 

VISUAL: Vídeo demonstrativo de ataque DDoS, visualização de tráfego malicioso, impacto em hosting e disponibilidade do site

 

[05:49] You have an opportunity to know that over a million people can hit a website at one time and it disables all of the other people who want to actually hit your website to even go to it because your hosting is going to crash.

 

[06:03] If your hosting crashes, Bob, dude, your page in the Google search results goes down to the very bottom. I don't want to press you or anything. I'm not trying to create problems, Bob.

 

[06:14] Seriously, this is your website. This is your online presence, and this matters a lot. Your competitors are doing it and I'm somebody who just ethically has a responsibility to follow up with you and let you know you need to take action.

 

[06:28] And I'm here to allow you to take action and we have to do it right away. When is it that we can talk? This is an urgent matter.

 

[06:36] What if somebody would do a DDoS attack literally right now? I'm not going to be the one. What if your competitor does it after they go to your website and they see that you don't have an SSL?

 

[06:46] It's just one example. Bob, I have so much more I can talk to you about here. Call me back. When can we talk? Talk soon. Bye.

 

[06:54] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Shoot & Cushion Sales Technique"

 

NÚMEROS: N/A

 

VISUAL: Analogia de "atirar na perna", metáfora médica de ferida/tratamento, processo de criar urgência e depois oferecer solução

 

[06:56] And simple videos like this give you an opportunity show a specific piece of data, press them from different angles. It's kind of like shooting them in the leg and then as you shoot them in the leg, you run up and you're like, "Oh, Jesus man, you got this wound on your leg right here. I'm going to cushion it a little bit."

 

[07:13] Aka in real time is you shoot somebody, what you're going to do to them is run up and you're going to acknowledge the wound, right?

 

[07:20] So, you can shoot them in the leg by bringing up a series of very drastic problems like getting shot in the leg to their business. Like, "Hey, an SSL, Bob. DDoS attacks would wipe you out and knock you out of Google in a second. What are you doing?" That shoots Bob in the leg.

 

[07:36] But then I'm like, "Oh, Bob. Jesus. Dude, that hurts." I'm patting him. I'm cushing. I'm like, "Dude, Bob, how'd you get shot, man? I can help you."

 

[07:46] And then you dig your thumb in there. You go deep. You get in and you in real time are saying to Bob, "Oh, Bob, Jesus, that's so painful. Oh, look how bad that hurts. Oh god. Bob, you got to do something about this. We got to stitch it up. It's going to get so infected."

 

[08:04] Aka after you're like, "Hey, Bob, listen. Like, this is an issue, but like it's going to be okay. Like, there's people who can solve this, but if you don't, Bob, your competitors in real time might actually come back to you and if they find out about this, dude, competitors know about stuff like that.

 

[08:22] And if you don't know about that, dude, one of my mentors tells me, you don't know what you don't know. And when you don't know it, takes advantage of you, Bob. I'm just here to help.

 

[08:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Doctor/Solution Provider Positioning"

 

NÚMEROS: N/A

 

VISUAL: Analogia médica completa - stitches (pontos), kit médico, antídoto, peroxide (água oxigenada), posicionamento como solução necessária

 

[08:34] But listen, and then you stitch it up. I'm here to be your doctor, dude. Okay? I got the stitches. I got my kit. I'm ready to go. I got my antidote. I'm ready for you, man. I got my peroxide. We're going to take care of this.

 

[08:48] But you got to allow me to take care of this, Bob. And I need you to get on the phone with me in order to do so. So, when is it that we can talk today? In the morning or in the afternoon?

 

[08:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Daily Action Plan"

 

NÚMEROS: 100 follow-ups

 

VISUAL: Call to action final, repetição massiva, foco em volume de atividade, mentalidade de "payday"

 

[09:00] Go do this a 100 times. Hit your numbers. Get your money up. And remember, it's green because it's payday, baby. Follow up. Schedule calls. Do your follow-up.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

193 tentativas de follow-up para fechar deal de $15K

Cliente pagando $25K para Gary Vee obtendo apenas 200-300 views por post

30 hashtags usando displaypurposes.com (começando com 3 hashtags base)

1 milhão+ de hits simultâneos em ataque DDoS podem derrubar um site

Meta de 100 follow-ups por dia

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Follow-up Criativo Data-Driven: Usar dados específicos e analytics para criar vídeos de follow-up personalizados

Ferramentas de Análise: SocialBlade, Instagram Analytics, Facebook Analytics, Twitter, YouTube, Spy Tools, SEO/PPC tools

Display Purposes: Sistema de 3 hashtags iniciais gerando 30 hashtags trending para Instagram

Técnica "Shoot & Cushion": Criar urgência com problema grave, reconhecer a dor, amplificar consequências, posicionar-se como solução

Analogia Médica: Posicionar-se como "doutor" que tem a cura para os problemas do prospect

Facebook Pixel: Implementar mesmo sem conhecimento de ads para coletar dados de tráfego

Competitive Intelligence: Usar falhas de concorrentes (até grandes nomes como Gary Vee) como prova

 

ESTRUTURA:

Assets Necessários: Smartphone, laptop/iPad/desktop, Excel sheet com números, boa iluminação, background profissional

Formato de Vídeo: Selfie video mostrando dados visuais em tela

Pain Points Técnicos: Ausência de SSL, falta de Facebook Pixel, vulnerabilidade a DDoS, baixo engagement em redes sociais

Script Framework: Reconhecimento de contatos anteriores → Apresentação de dados imparciais → Case studies/provas sociais → Urgência com consequências → Posicionamento como solução → Close com escolha binária (manhã ou tarde)

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Deal fechado: $15K após 193 follow-ups

Investimento desperdiçado do concorrente: $25K (Gary Vee) com resultados ruins

ROI potencial: Demonstração de que persistência (193 tentativas) justifica o investimento de tempo

 

DURAÇÃO: ~9 minutos FORMATO: Treinamento prático de vendas - Day 19 de série de desenvolvimento de follow-up
